description: American philosopher, psychologist, and educational reformer (1859–1952)
description_wiki: John Dewey (; October 20, 1859 – June 1, 1952) was an American philosopher, psychologist, and educational reformer.

description: public university in Berkeley, California, USA
description_wiki: The University of California, Berkeley (UC Berkeley, Berkeley, Cal, or California) is a public land-grant research university in the Southside and Northside neighborhoods of Berkeley, California, United States.

description: public library system in California, USA
description_wiki: The Berkeley Public Library is the public library system for Berkeley, California.

The Carnegie Corporation of New York is a private foundation established by Andrew Carnegie in 1911 "to promote the advancement and diffusion of knowledge and understanding."
Since its founding, the Carnegie Corporation has endowed or otherwise helped establish institutions including the United States National Research Council, Harvard University's Davis Center for Russian and Eurasian Studies (formerly known as the Russian Research Center), the Carnegie libraries, the University of Chicago Graduate Library School, and the Children's Television Workshop (now Sesame Workshop).
World War I, or the First World War (28 July 1914 – 11 November 1918), also known as The Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies (or Entente) and the Central Powers.

description: capital city of the United States of America
description_wiki: Washington, D.C., officially the District of Columbia and commonly known as simply Washington or D.C., is the capital city and federal district of the United States.

description_wiki: The English-Speaking Union (ESU) is an international educational membership organisation headquartered in London, England.

description: prison in the San Francisco Bay
description_wiki: United States Penitentiary, Alcatraz Island, also known simply as Alcatraz (English: , Spanish: [alkaˈtɾas] "the gannet") or The Rock, was a maximum security federal prison on Alcatraz Island, 1.25 miles (2.01 km) off the coast of San Francisco, California, United States.
